perturbation.original_text: Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'herein' from Section 10, it creates an in-text contradiction. Originally it specified 'except for the matters specifically agreed to herein', making it crystal clear only items in THIS agreement are being considered, but without the final word it implies that legal obligations can appear from other external factors creating confusion. The contract still specifies that there is no obligation without a definitive agreement, but the missing 'herein' clause leaves the agreement open to interpretations that obligations could arise from outside this specific agreement, conflicting with the intent that all obligations are explicitly stated in the current document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered by both Parties. Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to. Neither Party is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction, and either Party may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Party at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that legal obligations arise only from matters within 'this Agreement' ('herein'). The changed text removes 'herein', implying that legal obligations could arise from outside this specific agreement, contradicting the intent that all obligations are explicitly stated in the current document. The contradicted text reinforces this intent ('no contract or agreement...exists...unless and until a final definitive agreement'). By removing 'herein', the changed text weakens the agreement and opens it up to interpretations that legal obligations could appear from external factors, and therefore directly contradicts the original agreement's intent, fulfilling the conditions for 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of each Party and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of California applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within such state.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of each Party and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of California.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within such state', Section 13 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text limited the applicable laws to agreements fully within California. The altered text makes no such limitation, opening the agreement to interpretations involving laws governing agreements partially performed outside California. This creates uncertainty in enforcement because it is no longer clear which specific set of California laws apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is for the benefit of each Party and shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of California. 14. Severability . If any term, provision, covenant or restriction contained in this Agreement is held by any court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the terms, provisions, covenants or restrictions contained in this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidated, and if a covenant or provision is determined to be unenforceable by reason of its extent, duration, scope or otherwise, then the Parties intend and hereby request that the court or other authority making that determination shall only modify such extent, duration, scope or other provision to the extent necessary to make it enforceable and enforce them in their modified form for all purposes of this Agreement. 4
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly limited the agreement's governance to California laws applicable to agreements fully performed within the state. The changed text removes this limitation, broadening the scope to include California laws potentially governing agreements with partial performance outside the state. This directly contradicts the implicit limitation present in the original text and creates potential uncertainty in enforcement and interpretation. Therefore, it creates a strong in-text contradiction.
